← the Atlas

Dock — telling repos apart at a glance

feature · seedling ·implemented

The cards-mode dock groups rows by repo, but spends its whole colour budget on a per-branch rainbow and gives repo identity one 9.6px label that scrolls away. Move the colour onto the repo axis — a left-edge spine, a tinted/sticky header, or an avatar swatch — and calm the rows. Faithful prototypes of three treatments.

Contents

  • The two colour systems
  • Three repo treatments
  • The row rainbow
  • Recommendation

The dock already groups rows by repo. But you can’t see the grouping: the repo colour is one 9.6px label that scrolls away, while every row label is painted a different branch hue — a rainbow that fights the grouping it sits inside.

The two colour systems

buildTerminalDisplayInfos hands every render site two OKLCH hues — one per repo (repoColor), one per branch (branchColor, aliased annotationColor). Cards mode spends them backwards: repo gets a single tiny label, branch gets every row.

Three repo treatments

Same dock, repo colour moved onto a surface with real mass — and pinned so it survives the scroll. Rows calmed to neutral so the repo hue stops competing. The three differ only in how loud the repo signal gets; they compose.

The row rainbow

The other half of the fix is the rows. Keep them branch-coloured and the repo treatment still fights the rainbow; calm them to neutral and repo owns the hue outright (branch stays distinguishable by the state pip and, if wanted, a faint dot). Same treatment A, both ways:

Recommendation

Spine + sticky tinted header (branch colours kept)

Shipped: treatment A — #1379 . A continuous repo-colour spine down each section’s left edge plus a faintly repo-tinted sticky header — the smallest, most reversible change that puts a persistent repo cue on every row and keeps the label on screen as the rows scroll, without the visual weight of a full colour band. The per-row branch colours stay (the rainbow was kept by choice — it still tells branches apart within a repo); the spine sits at the section’s outer edge (x 0–3) and the active row’s existing 3px accent left-stripe (packages/client/src/canvas/dock/Dock.tsx:452) sits just inside it (x 3–6), so the two cues read together without overlapping. The same .dock-cards-section* treatment rides the mobile drawer, and rail mode already carries the repo colour in its section marks (packages/client/src/canvas/dock/Dock.tsx:523), so every dock surface shares one repo-identity vocabulary.
